title: UX polish — README quickstart, framework-history archive on init, accumulated punch-list sweep
description:
Sixth and final ticket of the wizard chain (TASK-010..015). The wizard now works end-to-end: clone → `node bin/init.js` → PROJECT.md → project-context.md → seeded backlog. But a non-technical operator opening the repo still has zero onboarding documentation, AND the seeded backlog lives alongside the framework's own 17+ historical tickets (TASK-001..TASK-017). This ticket closes both gaps and sweeps the small punch-list items that have accumulated from prior reviews.

## In scope

1. **README.md non-technical quickstart at repo root.** Currently there's no top-level README aimed at first-time operators. Write one that walks a non-technical user through: (a) what this framework is, in two sentences; (b) prerequisites (Node ≥20, git); (c) `node bin/init.js` and what to expect; (d) what the wizard produces (PROJECT.md, .claude/agents/project-context.md, seeded tickets); (e) how to ask Claude Code to start working on the first ticket. Keep it under 200 lines. No jargon — assume the reader has never heard of subagents or atomic writes.

2. **Framework-history archive on init.** When `bin/init.js` runs the `created` branch and detects pre-existing framework tickets in `tasks/` (heuristic: at least one ticket file exists AND none of them carry the `seed` label), prompt the user: `'Detected 17 pre-existing tickets that look like framework history. Archive them so this project starts fresh? [Y/n]'`. On 'Y' (or empty), move every `tasks/TASK-*.json` file to `.framework-history/tasks/` (creating the directory) and regenerate `tasks/index.json` empty. On 'n', leave alone (framework-dev mode). The `--force` flag implies 'Y'. The `already_initialized` branch does NOT trigger this. Add a `--no-archive` flag for non-interactive 'n'.

3. **Sweep TASK-010 LOWs.** `bin/new-task.js` has a dead-code `validate` on the title question (already covered by `required: true`) and a typo `engineerPrompter` (should be `enginePrompter`). Remove both, atomically.

4. **Sweep TASK-012 LOW.** `bin/init.js`'s argv parser silently ignores positional tokens; `bin/new-task.js` throws on any unrecognized token. Bring `bin/init.js` in line — throw with a clear message naming the unrecognized token.

## Out of scope

- Sweeping the deferred LOWs from TASK-013 (3 items) and TASK-014 (now spun into TASK-017). Those have explicit homes already.
- The TASK-014 reviewer's MEDIUMs — handled by TASK-009 AC7 and TASK-017.
- Designing a `gsd-update`-style mechanism for migrating an existing project to a newer framework version. Future ticket if/when relevant.
- Documenting every CLI flag exhaustively. README is a quickstart, not a reference manual.
acceptance_criteria: A new `README.md` at repo root walks a non-technical operator through clone → `node bin/init.js` → first ticket. Includes: framework one-liner, prerequisites, exact init command, expected wizard output, and the recommended first chat prompt for Claude Code. Under 200 lines. No mention of internal terms like `subagent`, `atomic write`, `bundle`, or `PROD map`.

`bin/init.js`'s `created` branch (when invoked on a repo with at least one `tasks/TASK-*.json` and zero of those tickets carry the `seed` label) prompts the user to archive the pre-existing tickets. Default response (empty input) is 'Y'. On 'Y', every matching ticket file is moved to `.framework-history/tasks/` (created if absent), and `tasks/index.json` is regenerated reflecting the empty store. On 'n', the tasks/ directory is left alone.

The archive prompt is suppressed when `--no-archive` is passed on the command line (implies 'n'). No flag suppresses-to-'Y' — the prompt default is already 'Y' on empty input, so a non-interactive 'archive yes' caller can pipe an empty line. `--force` controls PROJECT.md regeneration only and does NOT touch the archive decision (the `created` and `forced` branches are mutually exclusive — archive only fires in `created`).

The archive logic lives in a new exported function, `archiveFrameworkHistory({repoRoot, now})`, in either `src/backlog-seeder.js` or a new `src/framework-history.js` (developer's call — pick the simpler home). Function returns `{archived: <string[]>}` listing the moved ticket keys; returns `{archived: []}` when no framework history is detected (no pre-existing tickets OR any pre-existing ticket carries the `seed` label).

`bin/new-task.js`: the dead-code `validate` on the title question is removed (the `required: true` already covers empty-string rejection), AND the typo `engineerPrompter` is corrected to `enginePrompter`. All existing new-task tests continue to pass.

`bin/init.js`'s argv parser throws on any unrecognized positional token, with an error message that names the offending token (matching `bin/new-task.js`'s existing behavior). A regression test exercises the failure path.

Tests cover: (a) archiveFrameworkHistory with framework history present → returns the moved keys + on-disk files moved, (b) with seed-labeled tickets present → returns empty (no archive), (c) with empty tasks/ → returns empty, (d) bin/init.js integration: a created-branch init against a tmp repo with framework history triggers the archive after user confirms with empty input (default 'Y'), (e) --no-archive flag skips the prompt and leaves tasks/ alone, (f) `forced` branch (PROJECT.md exists, --force passed) does NOT touch tasks/, (g) bin/init.js argv strictness regression.

comments.author: orchestrator
comments.body: Created from the TASK-010 chain narrative (decision dated 2026-05-26T00:00:00Z). All gates cleared — TASK-014 closed at 2026-05-27T02:30:02Z. Final ticket of the six-ticket wizard chain. The framework-history archive is the riskier piece of the ticket (touches tasks/ semantics) but is necessary for the cloneable-by-non-technical-operator promise from the TASK-010 decision. Default-Y prompt keeps the happy path frictionless; --no-archive escape hatch preserves framework-dev mode. The archive only fires in the `created` branch — `already_initialized`, `forced`, and `resumed` all skip it (forced+archive would mean the user opted into both PROJECT.md regen AND archive, which is the same as a fresh start).

comments.author: orchestrator
comments.body: Reviewer verdict (fresh context): APPROVE-WITH-FOLLOWUPS. 0 HIGH, 0 MEDIUM, 3 LOW, 3 NIT — cleanest review of the wizard chain. Re-ran npm test from clean state twice: 155/155 pass, ~8.5s, no flakes. All 7 ACs traced AND probed out of band: AC1 README content verified to actually be readable by a non-technical operator (not just pass-the-tests); AC2-4 archive function passed all 6 probes (seed short-circuit no disk mutation, non-TASK files stay, empty tasks/ no archive dir, absent tasks/ no crash, corrupt ticket treated as framework history, identically-named pre-existing archive file silently clobbered by live); move op is renameSync (atomic same-volume); AC5 engineerPrompter→enginePrompter rename complete, dead title-validate removed, ac_count validate preserved; AC6 argv strict throws on stray positional; AC7 all test cases verified. The 3 LOWs are polish: (a) partial-archive failure mode — renameSync mid-loop has no rollback, so EACCES mid-archive leaves N of M tickets moved; (b) error message says 'unknown argument' vs bin/new-task.js 'unknown flag' — cosmetic divergence; (c) no on-screen post-archive confirmation (operator types Enter, next wizard question fires with no visual feedback that N tickets moved). The 3 NITs are even smaller. None block anything — deferred without follow-up ticket per the TASK-013/TASK-016 close pattern. Commits: 77fd667 (test) + c48c637 (impl).
